title: Anisotome haastii
taxonomy: Anisotome haastii is classified within the family Apiaceae, which includes other plants commonly known as caraways or parsley. This family is part of the larger order Apiales. The genus Anisotome is a group of flowering plants native to New Zealand. Anisotome haastii is a species within this genus and is endemic to New Zealand. It is a perennial herb, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same region.

intro: Anisotome haastii, also known as Haast's anisotome, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the carrot family Apiaceae. The health benefits of Anisotome haastii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as respiratory issues and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Anisotome haastii include sesquiterpenes, phenylpropanoids, and coumarins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Anisotome haastii include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions.
